title: Golem OpenClaw Next Execution Tranche
description: Select the single bounded execution tranche that best advances Golem/OpenClaw now that the sidecar project prioritization lane exists and frozen fronts are explicit.
question: Given the accepted sidecar browser lane, the frozen runtime boundaries, and the existing prioritization outputs, which single execution tranche should Golem/OpenClaw execute now for the highest return without reopening blocked fronts or touching live runtime?
expected_signals: use the sidecar project lanes as the main NOW front

sources.label: oc_cli
sources.notes: Public CLI surface for operator-facing baseline work.

sources.label: oc_channels
sources.notes: Public channels and routing surface for safe planning.

sources.label: oc_plugins
sources.notes: Public extension surface that can be kept as a secondary lane.

local_sources.label: local_capability_matrix
local_sources.notes: Versioned truth baseline for what is accepted, blocked or frozen.

local_sources.label: local_current_state
local_sources.notes: Current state and accepted lane stack.

local_sources.label: local_prioritization_lane
local_sources.notes: Defines the current front-level prioritization logic.

local_sources.label: local_runbook
local_sources.notes: Operational sidecar contract and command surface.

local_sources.label: local_handoff
local_sources.notes: Latest retome and lane stack summary.

local_sources.label: local_whatsapp_safety
local_sources.notes: Explicit frozen safety boundary that must stay out of scope.

decision_criteria.label: Project Return Now
decision_criteria.description: Favor tranches that convert the accepted sidecar lane into a direct project-facing baseline or ticket with visible return now.
decision_criteria.notes: The winner should move the main project, not just add commentary.

decision_criteria.label: Bounded Scope
decision_criteria.description: Favor tranches that stay small, have obvious borders, and do not collapse into a roadmap.
decision_criteria.notes: A tranche is only useful if it can stop cleanly.

decision_criteria.label: Safety Alignment
decision_criteria.description: Favor tranches that inherit the current frozen boundaries and do not re-open blocked or dangerous fronts.
decision_criteria.notes: The selected tranche must be safe by construction.

decision_criteria.label: Ticket Readiness
decision_criteria.description: Favor tranches that can be turned directly into one implementation ticket with explicit deliverables and verify.
decision_criteria.notes: This lane exists to produce something executable, not just to score fronts.

candidate_tranches.label: Gateway and Channels Public Baseline Pack
candidate_tranches.description: Use the accepted sidecar lane to build the next public baseline around CLI plus Channels and convert it into one implementation-ready ticket seed.
candidate_tranches.goal: Produce a bounded public baseline pack that clarifies how Golem should plan around OpenClaw CLI and channels next, without touching runtime.
candidate_tranches.relative_effort_note: Low because it stays on proven public docs plus versioned local truth and does not require new surfaces.
in_scope: Run a bounded sidecar dossier/decision/recommendation cycle on OpenClaw CLI and channels.
Produce one execution brief that turns the baseline into a single implementation ticket seed.
Keep WhatsApp, browser-native debt, workers and host control explicitly frozen.
out_of_scope: Any live runtime mutation, restart or channel reconfiguration.
WhatsApp reactivation or gateway work on the installed host.
Browser-native OpenClaw recovery, workers or host-control storytelling.
acceptance_criteria: Leaves one public baseline brief grounded in CLI plus channels, not a generic roadmap.
Leaves one ticket seed with deliverables, verify and kill criteria explicit.
Leaves frozen fronts and no-go boundaries visible in the final brief.
preconditions: Keep the sidecar browser lane as the only browser surface in use.
Treat WhatsApp and runtime changes as explicit no-go scope.
Use only public docs and versioned repo truth.
risk_hints: Can drift into broader product planning if CLI plus channels are not kept as the only baseline target.
Can under-specify the next ticket if the brief stops at recommendation instead of execution framing.
kill_criteria: Stop if the tranche grows beyond CLI plus channels baseline work.
Stop if runtime-touching work, login or live messaging becomes necessary.
Stop if the final brief cannot produce one bounded ticket seed better than the current prioritization output.
required_artifacts: CLI plus channels dossier artifact pair
CLI plus channels decision artifact pair
CLI plus channels recommendation artifact pair
One execution brief in markdown and json
verify_requirements: A tranche-specific verify or rerunnable command sequence proving the brief can be regenerated

implementation_ticket_seed.title: Build the CLI plus channels public baseline tranche
implementation_ticket_seed.summary: Run the accepted sidecar research stack over OpenClaw CLI and channels, then reduce it to one bounded execution brief and one implementation ticket.
deliverables: Public dossier over CLI plus channels
Decision and recommendation artifacts that justify the baseline
Execution brief with scope, verify and kill criteria
verify: Re-run the exact tranche command and compare the final brief artifacts

candidate_tranches.notes: Most direct way to turn the accepted sidecar lane into project movement.

candidate_tranches.label: Truth Surface Reentry Refresh
candidate_tranches.description: Tighten the repo truth package so the next implementation tranche starts with less ambiguity and less retome cost.
candidate_tranches.goal: Refresh the project truth surface so the next concrete execution ticket lands on clean boundaries and faster reentry.
candidate_tranches.relative_effort_note: Low because it is repo-local and heavily aligned with current versioned truth.
in_scope: Tighten CURRENT_STATE, handoff, lane docs and retome guidance after each verified tranche.
Keep verify entry points compact and easy to re-run.
out_of_scope: Creating a new runtime feature or public baseline by itself.
Broad doc churn disconnected from verified changes.
acceptance_criteria: Leaves a smaller, clearer reentry package than the current truth surface.
Keeps all updates tied to verified change rather than narration.
Does not replace a real project tranche with paperwork.
preconditions: Only write after verified outputs change.
Keep changes scoped to retome speed and truth alignment.
risk_hints: Can become paperwork if it is not anchored to one specific next tranche.
Can delay project-facing movement if taken before a more direct baseline tranche.
kill_criteria: Stop if the work stops reducing ambiguity for the next ticket.
Stop if docs start expanding without a paired verified delta.
required_artifacts: Updated truth docs with bounded delta
One small verify sequence proving the reentry path
verify_requirements: A short reentry checklist command set

implementation_ticket_seed.title: Refresh the truth surface for the next execution ticket
implementation_ticket_seed.summary: Trim and align the repo truth package so the next implementation tranche starts with one unambiguous set of boundaries and commands.
verify: Manual spot-check that the retome commands still match the repo

candidate_tranches.notes: High support value, but less direct project movement than a baseline pack.

candidate_tranches.label: Plugin Surface Reference Pack
candidate_tranches.description: Use the sidecar lane to package the plugin surface as the next planning reference without touching installed plugins or runtime.
candidate_tranches.goal: Clarify whether the plugin surface deserves the next implementation ticket or should stay behind the public baseline pack.
candidate_tranches.relative_effort_note: Medium because the surface is broader and easier to let drift into integration planning.
in_scope: Package the public plugin surface as a bounded planning reference.
Clarify when plugin work should follow the accepted baseline work.
out_of_scope: Installing plugins, enabling plugins or changing runtime config.
Community plugin experimentation on the live host.
Using plugins to bypass current frozen boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Leaves a compact plugin reference pack and one explicit reason it is not the first move.
Keeps plugin work as planning material, not runtime change.
preconditions: Treat plugin surface as a follow-on, not a replacement for baseline planning.
risk_hints: Can drift into speculative extension work before the baseline is settled.
Can invite scope growth through install or config temptation.
kill_criteria: Stop if install/config steps try to enter scope.
Stop if the tranche starts outrunning the CLI plus channels baseline case.
required_artifacts: Decision note on when plugin work should follow
One ticket seed or explicit defer note
verify_requirements: One rerun command that regenerates the plugin brief

implementation_ticket_seed.title: Build the plugin surface reference pack
implementation_ticket_seed.summary: Use public plugin docs to create a bounded planning reference and explicitly defer any install or runtime mutation.
deliverables: Decision note against early plugin expansion
verify: Re-run the tranche command and compare the resulting brief

candidate_tranches.notes: Useful runner-up surface, but easier to let grow sideways.
